meta.title: BMW X6 xDrive35i 40i Engine Replacement Cost UK
meta.description: Every BMW X6 petrol engine — N54, N55 and B58. E71, F16 and G06 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

@graph.name: BMW X6 xDrive35i / xDrive40i Engine Era Map
BMW X6 xDrive35i / xDrive40i Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X6 xDrive35i / xDrive40i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned engine typically costs £2,500–£8,500 supply only depending on generation, with the B58 sitting at £4,500–£8,500. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,600. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X6 xDrive35i / xDrive40i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the generation. The N54 (2008–2012) is rated ★★★☆☆ — HPFP failures are common. The N55 (2012–2016) is rated ★★★½☆ — more reliable but charge pipe and oil leaks are watchpoints. The B58 (2016+) is rated ★★★★☆ — one of BMW's most reliable modern engines.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X6 xDrive35i / xDrive40i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N54 HPFP failure, N55 charge pipe failure and oil leaks, and B58 water pump and cooling system issues are the most common. Carbon buildup affects all three engines.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X6 petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well-kept G06 X6 worth £25,000+, a £5,500–£8,500 recon is a manageable 20–35% of the vehicle's value — worth it. For an E71 worth £5,000–£10,000, assess carefully before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X6 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E71 2008–2012 is N54, E71 2012+ and F16 is N55, F16 2016+ and G06 is B58. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N54's HPFP, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — HPFP replacement is a straightforward repair costing £800–£1,500. If the pump has failed catastrophically and sent debris through the fuel system, a full fuel system overhaul may be required. At the first sign of long crank times or hesitation, book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.title: The X6 xDrive35i / xDrive40i THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Early X6 petrol — N54 with twin turbos; HPFP and wastegate rattle are defining issues.
The N55 replaced the N54 — improved HPFP reliability but charge pipe and oil leaks remain.
The modern B58 — BMW's most reliable modern six-cylinder; water pump and carbon buildup are the main watchpoints.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X6 xDrive35i / xDrive40i Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X6 engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X6 xDrive35i / xDrive40i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replace with updated pump; catch it before engine damage
Replace with upgraded metal pipe; eliminate the risk entirely
Walnut blasting; regular maintenance prevents severe buildup
Replace solenoids and seals; catch rough idle early
Wastegate actuator repair or turbo replacement
Rare but catastrophic; replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X6 Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * B58-powered G06 or late F16 (2016+) if you want the most reliable engine
* Documented HPFP replacement history on N54 cars
* Charge pipe upgraded to metal on N55 cars
* Full service history with regular oil changes
avoidIf: * N54 with long crank times or hesitation (HPFP risk)
* N55 with loss of power or whistling (charge pipe risk)
* Any engine with coolant loss and no diagnosis (water pump risk)
* Poor service history on any generation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X6 xDrive35i/xDrive40i badge spans three engine generations — and the reliability improves dramatically with each iteration. The N54 is a tuner's dream but a maintenance challenge. The N55 is a significant step forward. The B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern six-cylinders. Buy the newest B58 you can afford, and always demand documented service history.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X6 petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (wastegate or timing chain — B58 chain issues are rare)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N54 for pre-2012, N55 for 2012–2016, B58 for 2016+) — see the Era Map above
* HPFP Health — Long crank times? Hesitation? HPFP failure is a critical risk on N54
* Charge Pipe — Any loss of power or whistling? N55 charge pipe failure is common
* Coolant Level — Is the coolant low with no visible leak? Water pump risk on all engines
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil spots under the car or a burning oil smell (valve cover gaskets)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures; often point to carbon buildup or turbo issues

items.label: * Valvetrain
items.value: DOHC with Valvetronic and Double VANOS

items.label: Most Common Failure
items.value: HPFP failure (N54), charge pipe failure (N55), and water pump failure (all) — approximately 55% of N-series petrol enquiries [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Average Replacement Cost
items.value: £4,500 reconditioned, supply only [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

items.label: Peak Enquiry Season
items.value: Winter — cold starts expose HPFP and charge pipe issues [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Regional Demand
items.value: London (22%), South East (20%), South West (14%) [PATTERN DATA]
